model: Bayesian Depth-Confidence Model with Logistic Survival Functions
author: Ruqing Chen, GUT Geoservice Inc., Montreal, Canada

prior.description: Targeted-site base rate: fraction of exploration sites with surface anomalies that ultimately prove to host super-large (>=100 t Au) systems. Conservative estimate from Goldfarb et al. (2001) and Groves et al. (2003).

large_system.A_L_justification: Geometric intercept probability for 70-deg dipping, 50 m wide orebody at a targeted site with ~10x improved drill placement vs random.
large_system.H_c_L_justification: Critical closure depth for crustal-scale fault-valve systems. Empirically supported by Mponeng (4000 m) and LaRonde (3100 m). Conservative; could be 5000-6000 m.
large_system.k_L_justification: Steepness corresponding to ~880 m transition zone. Large systems have broad survival range.

small_system.A_S_justification: At targeted sites (selected for surface anomalies), ~20% of shallow drill holes encounter some sub-economic mineralization.
small_system.H_c_S_justification: Derived: H_paleo_crit (6000 m) minus Delta_erosion (5000 m) = 1000 m. Consistent with depth distribution of small orogenic occurrences in Abitibi (Robert & Poulsen, 2001).
small_system.k_S_justification: k = 0.005 yields transition zone Delta_Z ≈ 880 m, consistent with BDT thickness in Archean greenstone belts (Sibson, 1977; Scholz, 1988). Range 0.003-0.008 spans 550-1500 m.

paleo_depth_calibration.note: For terranes with different erosion histories, recalculate H_c_S accordingly.

H_2500m.interpretation: 97-fold amplification of 1% prior through depth-filtering alone

H_0m.interpretation: Shallow intercept: only 1-in-50 chance of super-large system

references.fault_valve: Sibson et al. (1988), Geology 16, 551-555; Sibson (2001), Rev. Econ. Geol. 14, 25-50
references.gold_deposits: Goldfarb et al. (2001), Ore Geol. Rev. 18, 1-75
